name: debug-cuda-crash
description: Tutorial for debugging CUDA crashes using API logging
capabilities: Debug CUDA crashes by capturing input tensors before failure
Detect NaN and Inf values using tensor statistics logging
Identify shape and dtype mismatches in kernel inputs
Use compute-sanitizer for memory access validation
Debug multi-GPU processes with separate log files
author: flashinfer-ai
securitySummary: All 160 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. This skill contains ONLY markdown documentation with zero executable code. The 'backtick execution' findings flag markdown code block formatting, not shell commands. 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings flag hash identifiers in metadata. 'System reconnaissance' findings flag environment variable documentation. Pure educational content for CUDA debugging.
valueStatement: CUDA crashes often leave no debugging information. This tutorial teaches you to use FlashInfer's API logging to capture tensor metadata before crashes. Learn to identify shape mismatches, dtype errors, and numerical issues quickly.
limitations: Requires FlashInfer library installed with @flashinfer_api decorator support
Logging must be enabled before running the code that crashes
Statistics logging (level 5) is skipped during CUDA graph capture
Does not fix crashes automatically, only aids diagnosis

useCases.title: Debug production ML crashes
useCases.description: Diagnose CUDA errors in production LLM serving workloads using API logging to capture failing inputs

useCases.title: Debug kernel memory errors
useCases.description: Use compute-sanitizer combined with API logging to find illegal memory access and out-of-bounds errors

useCases.title: Fix numerical instability
useCases.description: Detect NaN/Inf values in attention computations by examining tensor statistics from logged outputs

promptTemplates.title: Basic crash debugging
promptTemplates.prompt: My FlashInfer code crashed with 'illegal memory access'. Help me set up API logging to debug this.

promptTemplates.title: Numerical issues
promptTemplates.prompt: My attention kernel is producing NaN values. How do I use level 5 logging to find the source?

promptTemplates.title: Multi-GPU debugging
promptTemplates.prompt: My multi-GPU training job crashed. Show me how to log each rank separately for debugging.

promptTemplates.title: Memory errors
promptTemplates.prompt: Combine FlashInfer API logging with compute-sanitizer to debug memory access errors. Give me the exact command.

bestPractices: Always start with level 3 logging for tensor metadata without overwhelming output
Log to a file instead of stdout to preserve logs after crashes
Compare the last successful API call (inputs and outputs logged) with the first failed call (only inputs logged)
antiPatterns: Using level 5 logging by default (adds statistics overhead for every call)
Logging to stdout when program crashes (output may be lost)
Forgetting to disable logging in production (adds overhead even at level 1)

faq.question: What CUDA versions support API logging?
faq.answer: API logging works with any CUDA version supported by FlashInfer (CUDA 11.8+). The logging is implemented in Python.

faq.question: Does logging slow down my code?
faq.answer: Level 0 (disabled) has zero overhead. Level 1-3 add minimal overhead for metadata extraction.

faq.question: Can I use this with PyTorch torch.compile()?
faq.answer: Yes. API logging works with compiled PyTorch code. Statistics (level 5) are skipped during graph capture.

faq.question: Is my data safe in the log files?
faq.answer: Log files contain tensor shapes, dtypes, and statistics. They do NOT contain actual tensor values.

faq.question: What if no logs appear even with FLASHINFER_LOGLEVEL=3?
faq.answer: Verify the environment variable is set. Check that the API has the @flashinfer_api decorator.

faq.question: How is this different from CUDA's built-in tools?
faq.answer: API logging shows WHAT data caused the crash. Tools like compute-sanitizer show WHERE it occurred. Use both together.
